title: Decreased urine output in a 72-year-old post-op
presentation: A 72-year-old male with CKD stage 3, hypertension, and BPH is on post-op day 2 after elective hip replacement. Nursing notes that urine output has been less than 20mL/hr for the past 6 hours. He has been on ketorolac for pain and received gentamicin prophylaxis. Vitals: BP 142/88, HR 82, Temp 37.3C. Exam: mild lower extremity edema, bladder not palpable, Foley in place and patent.

gold_standard_workup.key_decision_point: FENa <1% = prerenal. FENa >2% = intrinsic renal. Muddy brown casts on UA = ATN. NSAIDs + gentamicin + CKD = high risk for ATN.
